meta.title: Performance and Caching
meta.excerpt: Core Web Vitals optimization, caching strategies, image optimization, and bundle analysis for the Next.js application.

seo.metaDescription: Core Web Vitals optimization and caching strategies

blocks.content: Comprehensive performance optimization documentation covering Core Web Vitals targets, caching strategies, image optimization, code splitting, and bundle analysis.

blocks.title: Largest Contentful Paint (LCP)
blocks.content:
Target: < 2.5 seconds

Measures when the largest visible element loads.

blocks.title: First Input Delay (FID)
blocks.content:
Target: < 100 milliseconds

Measures responsiveness to user input.

items: Break up long tasks into smaller chunks

blocks.title: Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS)
blocks.content:
Target: < 0.1

Measures visual stability during loading.

items: Avoid inserting content above existing content
Use font-display: swap for web fonts

blocks.title: Next.js Built-in Caching
blocks.content: Use Data Cache, Request Deduplication, Request Memoization, Router Cache, and Full Route Cache to reduce load time and API pressure.

items: Data Cache for static and revalidated fetches
Request Deduplication for repeated server requests
Request Memoization during a single render pass
Full Route Cache for static routes
Use Next Image for responsive delivery and automatic optimization
Always specify width and height to prevent layout shift

blocks.title: Dynamic Import Pattern

items: Load heavy features only when needed
Show skeletons during async component loading
Use route-based splitting from Next.js app router
Set performance budgets and alert on regressions
Keep main bundle under 50KB gzipped when possible
Collect Web Vitals from real users
Integrate error tracking and performance traces
Run regular PageSpeed and Lighthouse audits
